title: TPU v1 · Proving the systolic array is correct: an independent naive matrix-multiply Golden Model with elementwise Scoreboard checks
oneLine: Verification is self-checking, not visual waveform inspection. Feed identical stimulus to the RTL array and a separately written naive matrix-multiply Golden Model; the Scoreboard compares every output element, passing exact matches and immediately reporting the failing (i,j). The essential rule is independent implementation: if the model copies the RTL's interpretation, both can share one defect and produce a green regression despite a real bug—a common-mode error.

legend.name: DUT: systolic-array RTL under verification
legend.desc: The 256×256 weight-stationary array RTL: weights remain local, activations flow cycle by cycle, and partial sums accumulate down columns into 32-bit results. It is the implementation whose correctness must be demonstrated, never assumed.

legend.name: Golden Reference Model (independent implementation)
legend.desc: A separate, hardware-independent naive matrix multiply using three nested loops to compute C=A×B element by element. It cares only about the mathematical result, not systolic timing or pipelines. Its C[i][j] values are the expected answers. The defining property is independence.

legend.name: Self-checking Scoreboard
legend.desc: Compares DUT observations and Golden Model expectations element by element for identical stimulus. All equal → PASS; any mismatch → immediate FAIL with (i,j), expected value, and observed value. It localizes the failing accumulation without manual waveform inspection.

legend.name: Independent implementation prevents common-mode blind spots
legend.desc: The most dangerous model error is reproducing RTL logic or the same pseudocode for convenience. Then any misunderstanding appears on both sides, the Scoreboard reports PASS, and a real defect escapes. Implement the Golden Model from the requirements, ideally by another person and with a different approach.

steps.cap: ① One stimulus, two independent paths: send the same activations a and weights to both the DUT array and the Golden matrix-multiply model, each computing independently.
② The DUT follows hardware behavior—stationary weights and cycle-by-cycle accumulation—to produce 256 columns of 32-bit observed results.
③ The Golden Model directly computes C=A×B with three nested loops using bit-accurate integer arithmetic and no floating point, producing expected results.
④ The Scoreboard compares observed and expected values element by element: match passes; mismatch triggers $error with (i,j), expected, and observed values, localizing the PE.
⑤ Enforce independence: the Golden Model must not copy the RTL's interpretation. Shared defects that leave the Scoreboard green are the most dangerous common-mode blind spot.

before: Choose the Reference Model's source of truth: derive the Golden Model from requirements and the mathematical definition C=A×B with integer accumulation, not from RTL design documents. State this explicitly in the plan to prevent a common-mode blind spot at its source.

Choose bit-accurate versus bit-approximate behavior: INT8 inference uses integer MACs, with 8b×8b accumulated into 32b without rounding. The Golden Model should therefore be bit-accurate and comparisons strictly equal rather than tolerance-based. Fix this criterion before debates about whether a difference of 1 is acceptable.

Define stimulus and completion criteria: include directed all-zero, full ±127, identity, long vectors that push 32b accumulation into high bits (K=256 terms, magnitude about 2^22), and negative-weight cases, plus many random matrices. Completion requires coverage closure and 0 regression failures.

Specify Scoreboard diagnostics: PASS/FAIL alone is insufficient. Print failing (i,j), expected value, observed value, and current stimulus. Verification must both detect an error and quickly localize the PE or accumulation segment.
during: First rule: the Golden Model must be independently implemented. Ideally another engineer writes it from requirements using a different method—naive nested loops rather than systolic behavior. Copying the RTL interpretation creates a common-mode error: the DUT and model agree while both are wrong, the deadliest self-checking failure.

Second rule: feed exactly the same stimulus to both paths. DUT and Golden Model must receive the identical a values, weights, and operation. Otherwise they solve different problems and neither PASS nor FAIL is meaningful. Use one stimulus source and fan it out.

Align timing: the array is delayed, the model is immediate. Systolic results emerge after pipeline latency, while the model computes immediately. Match by operation through a transaction id rather than raw cycle number so each observed tile pairs with its own expectation.

Compare element by element, not only a whole-result digest. Check all 256×256 C[i][j] values and localize the first failure. A hash reports only that something differs; elementwise comparison identifies the exact row and column accumulator.

Illustrative SystemVerilog Scoreboard snippet, not private TPU code: foreach(C[i][j]) if (dut_C[i][j] !== gold_C[i][j]) $error("mismatch (%0d,%0d) exp=%0d got=%0d", i, j, gold_C[i][j], dut_C[i][j]); Four-state !== also rejects X/Z.

Scope note: Scoreboards, Golden Models, and independent implementation against common-mode errors are standard digital-verification methodology. TPU's private environment and scripts are not public; the documented 256×256 weight-stationary INT8 array and 32b accumulation are only the DUT context.

result.label: Identical source for two paths
result.value: Same a values / weights → DUT array + Golden Model

result.label: Alignment
result.value: Pair observed and expected results by transaction id, not raw cycle

result.label: Verdict
result.value: Every element equal → PASS; any mismatch → FAIL with localization

after: Check coverage convergence: a green Scoreboard means only that executed tests passed. Inspect covergroups for boundaries, negative weights, accumulation peaks, and array sizes. Green without coverage target is not complete.

Test the Scoreboard itself by injecting a known bug: deliberately flip one RTL bit or feed the Golden Model altered data, then confirm the Scoreboard reports FAIL at the correct location. A checker that always passes is more dangerous than none.
Add every failure to regression: after diagnosing and fixing a mismatch, retain its stimulus as a directed regression case; record random seeds for exact reproduction and bug convergence.

Sign off on bug convergence: run long random regressions until new-defect rate approaches zero, coverage meets target, and directed + random suites have 0 failures. Archive the Golden Model and coverage report with signoff.

Freeze the pre-synthesis baseline: hand off self-checked RTL, the Golden Reference Model, and regression suite. Reuse the same Golden Model for post-synthesis and post-layout netlist regression to confirm tools did not alter functionality.
source: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a TPU, Jouppi et al., ISCA 2017 (TPU v1's 256×256 weight-stationary INT8 array and 32-bit accumulation are documented; Scoreboards, Golden Reference Models, and independent implementation to prevent common-mode errors are standard verification practice, not TPU v1 disclosure)
